related_questions.question: What is the cheapest way to do a will in BC?
related_questions.snippet: What Is the Cheapest Way to Do a Will in BC? The most affordable option is often to prepare your will using an online platform or documents online to create legal documents, but it must still meet all legal requirements to be legally valid under provincial laws.
related_questions.title: How Much Does It Cost to Make a Will in BC? - Onyx Law Group

related_questions.question: How much will a solicitor charge for probate?
related_questions.snippet: Solicitors' Probate fees are usually based on guidance from the Law Society which sets an initial fee of 0.75% of the value of the property, plus 1.5% of the value of other assets, and other charges on top of that.
related_questions.title: Solicitors' Probate Fees: | Ola Leslie Solicitors Lower Marsh SE1

related_questions.question: What not to do during separation?

text_blocks.snippet: During separation, you should not make rash decisions, badmouth your ex (especially to kids or online), rush into new relationships, hide assets, or involve children in disputes; instead, focus on maintaining the status quo financially and co-parenting responsibly while seeking legal advice to protect yourself and your family.
snippet_highlighted_words: make rash decisions, badmouth your ex (especially to kids or online), rush into new relationships, hide assets, or involve children in disputes

list.snippet: Draining joint accounts: Don't empty joint accounts; take only your half to avoid legal issues, according to Avvo.
Taking on new debt or making large purchases: Avoid significant new debt or buying expensive items like new property, as this complicates asset division, says Tonkin Legal Group.
Hiding assets: Don't conceal income or assets, as this can backfire in court.
Emotional outbursts: Don't make major decisions driven by anger or rush to file divorce papers; let emotions cool, say Michael West Law.
Badmouthing your ex: Avoid speaking ill of your former partner, especially in front of children, as it can harm your case, notes Cordell & Cordell.
Social media drama: Don't post about your situation or grievances online.
Rushing new relationships: Avoid jumping into a rebound relationship too quickly.
Controlling or hovering: Don't track or control your ex's activities, as this erodes trust, according to Marriage Helper.

list.title: Involving children:
list.snippet: Don't use kids as messengers or involve them in adult disputes.

list.title: Blocking contact with kids:
list.snippet: Don't deny your ex time with the children; aim for co-parenting, says Michael West Law.

list.title: Moving out prematurely:
list.snippet: Unless for safety, staying in the home can be better, say parents4children.com.

list.title: Lying to lawyers/court:
list.snippet: Be honest with your attorney and the court; don't lie or destroy evidence, says Memphis Divorce.
Maintain routines: Stick to your normal routines where possible.
Prioritize health: Exercise, eat well, and don't use substances to cope.
Seek support: Talk to lawyers, therapists, or mediators; don't try to handle it alone.

related_questions.question: What are the three types of separation?

text_blocks.snippet: Three common separation processes are Filtration (separating solids from liquids using a filter), Distillation (separating liquids by boiling points), and Evaporation/Crystallization (removing liquid to leave a solid, often salt). Other key methods include chromatography, magnetic separation, and absorption/stripping, relying on differences in size, magnetism, or solubility.
snippet_highlighted_words: Filtration (separating solids from liquids using a filter), Distillation (separating liquids by boiling points), and Evaporation/Crystallization (removing liquid to leave a solid, often salt)

list.snippet: Filtration: Separates insoluble solids from liquids using a filter medium (like paper) that traps the solid but lets the liquid pass through (e.g., sand from water).

Distillation: Separates liquids with different boiling points by heating the mixture, vaporizing the lower-boiling liquid, and then condensing it back into a separate container (e.g., alcohol from water).
Evaporation & Crystallization: Removes a liquid solvent (usually water) by heating, leaving behind the dissolved solid solute, often in crystalline form (e.g., salt from saltwater).
Chromatography: Separates components based on their different affinities for a stationary medium and a mobile solvent (e.g., separating ink colors).
Magnetic Separation: Uses a magnet to pull out magnetic materials from non-magnetic ones (e.g., iron filings from sand).
Absorption/Stripping: Moves components between gas and liquid phases, often using chemical reactions or solubility differences.

related_questions.question: Can you do a legal separation without a lawyer?
related_questions.snippet: Although parties can draw up a separation agreement without the assistance of lawyers, it is often risky to do so. Without knowledge of their legal rights, the parties may draft an agreement that can create problems in the future or fail to address all of the issues between them.
related_questions.title: Separation Agreements - People's Law Library

related_questions.question: What is cheaper, divorce or separation?
related_questions.snippet: Divorce can be costly. Legal costs can add up as both partners may need attorneys to sort through paperwork, divide assets or determine custody of children. Legal separation could be cheaper than divorce, but laws can vary from state to state and greatly affect the cost of these two distinct legal processes.
related_questions.title: Is a Legal Separation Cheaper Than a Divorce? - Experian

related_questions.question: What is the average spousal support payment in BC?
related_questions.snippet: The spousal support amount ranges from 1.5 to 2 percent of the difference between the spouses' gross incomes for each year of the relationship up to a maximum of 50 percent. For marriages of 25 years or longer, the range is from 37.5 to 50 percent of the income difference, practically equalizing the incomes.
related_questions.title: How is Spousal Support / Alimony Calculated in British Columbia?

related_questions.question: Do you pay tax on inheritance in BC?
related_questions.snippet: British Columbia does not impose an inheritance tax, and across Canada, there is no inheritance tax. The BC government followed the federal government, getting rid of gift taxes and succession duties in BC in 1977.
related_questions.title: Inheritance Tax BC: What You Need to Know in 2025

related_questions.question: What is the biggest mistake with wills?
related_questions.snippet: “The biggest mistake people have when it comes to doing wills or estate plans is their failure to update those documents. There are certain life events that require the documents to be updated, such as marriage, divorce, births of children.
related_questions.title: The 7 Biggest Mistakes People Make In Their Wills, According To Estate ...
